summary: Pre-clinical project proposal for antisense oligonucleotide (AON) therapeutics targeting FOXO3 isoforms to treat degenerative disc disease. Phase 1 funding approved (~$298K) for AON design and 2D culture validation. No experimental results yet - this is a project plan document describing proposed work, not completed studies.

content_blocks.title: Project Overview & Scientific Foundation
content_blocks.content: Project Title: FOXO3. Therapeutic approach: Antisense oligonucleotides (AONs) to modulate FOXO3 gene by shifting expression toward health-promoting FOXO3-long isoform and away from FOXO3-short isoform that accumulates during aging. Discovery by Prof. Lorna Harries' lab: association between extreme lifespan and FOXO3 genetic variation is mediated by alternatively expressed mRNA isoforms. FOXO3-long encodes complete forkhead DNA-binding domain (ubiquitous), while FOXO3-short lacks key regulatory motifs and is enriched in aging muscle and degenerating tissues. Strategy: restore youthful long-to-short ratio using steric hindrance ASOs. Target indication: Degenerative Disc Disease (DDD)/Intervertebral Disc Degeneration (IDD) - most common cause of chronic lower back pain. Secondary/future indications: sarcopenia, cardiovascular disease, neurodegenerative disorders, autoimmune/immune dysfunction, cancer, diabetes complications.

content_blocks.title: Patient Population Size & Market
content_blocks.content: DDD/Lower back pain: leading cause of physical disability worldwide. 12-16% of adults visit GP annually. UK alone: 3.5M incident cases, 17.3M prevalent cases annually, 116M production days lost per year. Global spine-pain therapeutics/surgery market: ~$35B (2025), projected ~$45B by 2029 (7.5% CAGR). Broader FOXO3-associated disease markets: cardiovascular ($160B), cancer ($200B), autoimmune ($170B), neurodegeneration ($20B), diabetes ($40B), sarcopenia ($4B projected).

content_blocks.title: Mechanism of Action
content_blocks.content: Antisense oligonucleotides (AONs) using steric hindrance translation blockers target isoform-specific translation start sites to modulate FOXO3 isoform expression: (1) Block FOXO3-short isoform translation → shifts balance toward FOXO3-long; (2) Block negative regulatory elements specific to FOXO3-long → enhances full-length expression; (3) FOXO3-long protein translocates to nucleus and transactivates target genes involved in autophagy, mitochondrial quality control, DNA-damage repair, metabolic flexibility, inflammatory restraint; (4) In DDD context: FOXO3 overexpression ameliorates intervertebral disc degeneration by decreasing NLRP3-mediated pyroptosis (cell death) and reducing extracellular matrix degradation; (5) Local intradiscal injection delivers naked oligonucleotides directly to target tissue, with long duration of action (up to 6 months for some ASOs).

content_blocks.title: Biological Target
content_blocks.content: FOXO3 (Forkhead box O-3) transcription factor, specifically: (1) FOXO3-long isoform (full-length, complete forkhead DNA-binding domain) - target for enhancement; (2) FOXO3-short isoform (truncated, lacks key regulatory motifs) - target for suppression; (3) rs2802292 G-allele (longevity-associated SNP) associated with ~36% increased chance of exceptional longevity across >11 human populations, 1.9-fold increased probability of living past 95 years; (4) FOXO3 orchestrates downstream pathways: autophagy, mitochondrial maintenance, oxidative stress response (SOD2, CAT), DNA damage repair (GADD45A), anti-inflammatory signaling, cellular senescence regulation.

content_blocks.title: Current Development Stage
content_blocks.content: Pre-clinical discovery phase. Project funding approved (based on VDP-146 revision incorporating community feedback). Phase 1 work initiated: AON design and validation in monolayer culture (Milestone 1a), evaluation of AON efficacy in 2D culture models of DDD (Milestone 1b). Go/No-Go decision point after Year 1. Phase 2 (ex vivo human organotypic models) contingent on successful achievement of Phase 1 milestones. NO EXPERIMENTAL RESULTS REPORTED - this is a project proposal describing planned work.

content_blocks.title: Development Timeline & Milestones
content_blocks.content: Phase 1 (Current Fundraise - ~$298K): Milestone 1a: AON design and validation in monolayer culture (NHDF, HNPC, HAFC cells) - identify ≥2 lead AON candidates; Milestone 1b: Evaluate AON efficacy in 2D culture - demonstrate functional rescue of DDD phenotypes, impact on senescence, autophagy, mitochondrial function; → Go/No-Go Point after Year 1; Milestone 2: Evaluate AON efficacy in human ex vivo organ-on-chip models and primary IVD explants. Phase 2 (Subsequent Fundraise): Oligo sequence and chemistry optimization; In vivo PK/PD and toxicology studies in rodent DDD models (CRO); CMC and safety toxicology package; Path to IND filing.

content_blocks.title: Team Information
content_blocks.content: Key Personnel: Professor Lorna Harries, PhD - Principal Investigator; Postdoctoral Research Associate (PDRA) - To be appointed for this project. Affiliations: University of Exeter Medical School, Exeter, UK (Harries Lab - RNA-mediated disease mechanisms group); SENISCA Ltd, Exeter, UK (Spin-out company; Harries is Founder & CSO).

content_blocks.title: Team Expertise Areas
content_blocks.content: RNA biology and oligonucleotide drug design (30 years' experience); Antisense oligonucleotide delivery by gymnosis (transfection-free); Cellular senescence biology and aging research; High-content imaging and machine learning for senescent cell quantification; Primary cell culture (fibroblasts, nucleus pulposus cells, annulus fibrosus cells); Multi-omics approaches to aging; Organ-on-chip systems and 3D tissue models; Drug discovery and translational research (SENISCA pipeline experience); 160+ peer-reviewed publications, H-index 56, 20,000+ citations.

content_blocks.title: Intellectual Property Status
content_blocks.content: Patent Application Status: IP to be generated. Phase 1 output will provide sufficient data for initial patent filing covering prototype oligonucleotides demonstrating FOXO3 isoform modulation and attenuation of senescence/disease markers in 2D/3D tissue culture systems. Claims to be bolstered with in vivo data within 12 months post-filing (contingent on Phase 2 funding). IP ownership to VitaDAO (via Vitality Now, Swiss association) per revised funding terms. Scope of IP Protection: Composition of matter for antisense oligonucleotides targeting FOXO3 isoform-specific sequences; Methods of use for modulating FOXO3-long/short isoform ratios; Methods of treating degenerative disc disease and other FOXO3-associated conditions; Oligonucleotide chemistries and delivery formulations optimized for local injection.

content_blocks.title: Freedom-to-Operate Assessment
content_blocks.content: Strong FTO position. FOXO3 has never been successfully pharmacologically targeted despite strong genetic validation. Previous agonists (e.g., trehalose) had pleiotropic effects on AKT, PI3K, AMPK pathways and lacked FOXO3 specificity. The isoform-specific ASO approach represents a novel mechanism distinct from prior art. Oligonucleotide therapeutics have 4 FDA approvals in 2023 alone (fastest growing drug modality). PI has proven track record with SENISCA's oligonucleotide portfolio. Competitive landscape for DDD includes non-disease-modifying approaches (hydrogels), non-specific pathway targeting (TGFβ), and biologics (stem cells) - targeted AONs would differentiate on precision, safety, and duration of action.

key_findings: PRE-CLINICAL PROJECT PROPOSAL - NO EXPERIMENTAL DATA YET. Phase 1 work just initiated.
Antisense oligonucleotide (AON) approach to modulate FOXO3 isoforms (long vs short) for treating degenerative disc disease (DDD)
Target validation: FOXO3 rs2802292 G-allele associated with 36% increased exceptional longevity, 1.9-fold probability of living past 95 years across >11 populations

Mechanism: Block FOXO3-short translation and negative regulatory elements → shift toward FOXO3-long → nuclear translocation → transactivation of autophagy, mitochondrial quality control, DNA repair, anti-inflammatory pathways

Phase 1 milestones (~$298K funding): (1) AON design/validation in monolayer culture (NHDF, HNPC, HAFC cells) - identify ≥2 lead candidates; (2) Evaluate efficacy in 2D DDD models - demonstrate rescue of phenotypes, impact on senescence, autophagy, mitochondrial function
Go/No-Go decision after Year 1. Phase 2 contingent on success: ex vivo organ-on-chip models, primary IVD explants
Future Phase 2: oligo optimization, in vivo PK/PD/toxicology in rodent DDD models (CRO), CMC/safety package, path to IND
Delivery: local intradiscal injection of naked oligonucleotides, duration up to 6 months
Team: Prof. Lorna Harries (PI, University of Exeter, SENISCA Founder/CSO), 30 years oligonucleotide experience, 160+ publications, H-index 56
IP: To be generated from Phase 1 data. Patent filing planned covering AON sequences, isoform modulation methods, treatment methods for DDD and FOXO3-associated conditions
Market: DDD/spine pain ~$35B (2025) → $45B (2029). Broader FOXO3 markets: cardiovascular $160B, cancer $200B, autoimmune $170B
Strong FTO - FOXO3 never successfully targeted pharmacologically before. Isoform-specific ASO approach distinct from prior art
